name: Can I sue Perplexity if I'm a small blogger and not the New York Times?
description: Absolutely, size doesn't matter; prove copying and consult an IP lawyer.[1]

mainEntity.name: Can I sue Perplexity if I'm a small blogger and not the New York Times?

acceptedAnswer.text: Absolutely, size doesn't matter; prove copying and consult an IP lawyer.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, any copyright holder can sue for infringement regardless of size if substantial copying occurs.[1] The NYT case sets precedent for scraping and output reproduction claims applicable to creators universally.[1] Key factors: ownership proof, amount/substantiality copied, market harm, and fair use rejection.[1] Gather query examples showing your content in responses; small claims court may suffice for modest damages. This levels the field against AI giants.
